meta.optimizer_fix_note: Interpreter O1-O3 numbers (nxl_O*_ms) below were measured before the interpreter_mode fix (commit b7cbb32+). Pre-fix O3 was 35% slower than O0 because TypeSpecialization/FunctionInlining/LoopUnrolling/DispatchOptimization passes were running in interpreter mode. Post-fix, interpreter_mode=True restricts the pipeline to ConstantFolding+DCE+StrengthReduction+CSE+TCO only. The compiled_O0/O3_ms numbers are unaffected (they use the LLVM backend, not the interpreter pipeline).

optimization_description.O1: Interpreter: constant folding + DCE; Compiled: +dispatch hints +string interning
optimization_description.O2: Interpreter: O1 + strength reduction; Compiled: +loop unrolling +inlining +type specialization
optimization_description.O3: Interpreter: O2 + CSE + TCO; Compiled: +aggressive inlining +all NexusLang opts

meta.compiled_benchmark_methodology: All process-spawn timings (subprocess.run). interpreter_subprocess_ms includes Python 3.14 startup (~200ms for cold import). c_o3_internal_ms is the computation-only time reported by the C binary's internal timer. compiled_*_ms is the full process time of the compiled NexusLang binary (mostly OS process startup for small workloads).

fibonacci_iterative.note: Fibonacci(1000) is sub-microsecond in C; NexusLang compiled binary process time (~0.75ms) is dominated by OS process startup overhead. Speedup vs interpreter is accurate at 311x.

matrix_sum.note: Matrix(200x200) inner computation is ~0.1us in C; NexusLang compiled process time (~0.73ms) is OS startup dominated. Interpreter speedup 3426x is the key validated metric.

sieve_of_eratosthenes.description: Prime sieve up to 1000 - array access, conditional branching
sieve_of_eratosthenes.note: Sieve(1000) has non-trivial loop work (16.8us in C). Compiled NexusLang is 51x slower than C pure compute, 2884x faster than NexusLang interpreter. Output corrected from '999' (stale) to '168 primes'.

prime_count_naive_200.description: Naive prime counting up to 200 -- divisibility check loops
